articles.title: Kerala polls: Pinarayi hits back at Rahul Gandhi over Sabarimala gold row, questions accused’s link to Sonia Gandhi
articles.description: Chief Minister’s reaction comes a day after Rahul Gandhi sang a line from a popular parody song about CPI(M) leaders’ alleged roles in the gold theft at the Sabarimala temple

articles.title: Israel says four soldiers killed as army pushes deeper into south Lebanon
articles.description: Hezbollah attempts to make Lebanon ground invasion 'costly' for Israeli army as it continues its advance.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: party manifestos
articles.description: This is a compilation of poll manifestos released by prominent political parties in Tamil Nadu ahead of the State Assembly election scheduled to be held on April 23, 2026

articles.title: T.N. Assembly election: Tamil Nadu needs to be saved from the DMK, says Vijay
articles.description: The TVK president criticises the government for failing to provide safety and employment opportunities, and calls the ruling party an ‘evil force’

articles.title: Four policemen injured in a blast in Challapalli police station
articles.description: The condition of three of the injured is stated to be critical, the police said

articles.title: BJD MP quits House panel protesting disparaging remarks against Biju Patnaik
articles.description: BJP MP and chairman of Parliamentary Standing Committee on IT and Communications Nishikant Dubey alleged that former Odisha Chief Minister Biju Patnaik worked as a “link” between Indian government and the CIA

articles.title: Poomedai Ramaiah: a lone voice amid the electoral cacophony
articles.description: A short story by writer Jeyamohan seeks to capture the character of Poomedai Ramaiah, an Independent candidate in many elections, who would move around the streets of Nagercoil on his bicycle, putting up posters that minced no words about local politics. He died in 1996

articles.title: Quote of the Day: Elon Musk says AI will surpass human intelligence by end of 2026 — What it means
articles.description: Elon Musk's boldest prediction yet: AI beats human intelligence by 2026. Everything you need to know about the world's richest tech billionaire.

articles.description: The White House says US policy toward Cuba remains unchanged despite allowing a sanctioned Russian tanker.

topStories.title: The Silicon Valley congressional race is getting ugly
topStories.description: The primary isn’t until early June, but the CA-17 race between five-term incumbent Ro Khanna and tech founder Ethan Agarwal is already getting nasty. Agarwal entered the race in March, backed by a roster of prominent tech billionaires largely in response to Khanna’s public support for a proposed California ballot measure that would impose a […]

topStories.title: Quote of the day by Abraham Lincoln: ‘The best way to predict your future is…’
topStories.description: Abraham Lincoln, a pivotal figure in American history, rose from humble beginnings to become 16th President. His leadership during the Civil War and commitment to abolishing slavery left a lasting legacy of resilience and justice, emphasising the importance of active effort in shaping the future.

topStories.title: Florida airport to be renamed after Trump — From money to iconic venues, US President's naming spree continues
topStories.description: With the name change, the Florida airport becomes the latest in a series of buildings, institutions, and money, among other things, to bear Donald Trump's name. Here's a look at some notable entities bearing the US President's name or likeness.

topStories.title: Dollar surges in Asia as war's safe-haven shelter
topStories.description: The US dollar is surging, becoming the strongest safe asset. War in the Middle East has sent oil prices soaring and other assets tumbling. This situation raises fears of a global recession. The dollar's strength is supported by its status as an energy exporter and rising US Treasury yields. Asian currencies are experiencing significant losses.

topStories.title: Iran-US war: US reveals why they killed Iranian leaders, Leavitt says ‘lied to United States’
topStories.description: The U.S. adopts a tougher stance on Iran, viewing past negotiations as deceptive.

topStories.title: LeBron James records triple-double as Lakers dominate Wizards
topStories.description: Led by James's 125th regular-season triple-double, the LA Lakers destroyed the Washington Wizards for their 49th win.

topStories.title: Gold, silver prices rise 1% but analysts cautious after 15% March drop; here's why
topStories.description: Gold and silver prices rebounded in global markets after a sharp March sell-off, supported by a weaker dollar and easing bond yields. However, sentiment remains cautious as inflation risks, elevated energy prices, and fading expectations of US rate cuts continue to weigh on outlook and keep volatility elevated.

topStories.title: Spain closes airspace to US warplanes involved in war on Iran
topStories.description: Spain has taken another step in rejecting the US-Israeli war on Iran, denying US warplanes the use of its airspace.

topStories.title: What we learned from Al Jazeera’s interview with Marco Rubio
topStories.description: Experts cast doubt on some of the claims made by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio in an interview with Al Jazeera.

topStories.title: Why Trump faces an agonizing decision on obliterating Iran’s oil supply if he can’t get a deal
topStories.description: Trump threatens to obliterate Iran's oil and power infrastructure on Truth Social while envoys reportedly pursue a nuclear deal through back-channel talks.

topStories.title: Florida county's Democratic Party chair accused of hitting man in head with bullhorn at 'No Kings' protest
topStories.description: The Hernando County Democratic Party chair was arrested for allegedly striking a disabled veteran with a bullhorn at a "No Kings" protest over the weekend.

dailyNews.title: Last day of Amazon's Big Spring Sale: Get up to 88% off Apple, DeWalt and more while you can
dailyNews.description: Save on a tire inflator that's down to one of its lowest prices ever, a sports bra that's nearly 80% off and more through tomorrow, March 31.

dailyNews.title: Shedeur Sanders ditches rookie number as Browns announce change heading into 2026 season
dailyNews.description: Shedeur Sanders is switching to No. 2 with the Cleveland Browns, reclaiming his college number from Jackson State and Colorado for his sophomore NFL season.

dailyNews.title: Teacher of the Year finalist gets 14 years for sexual relationship with student she kept contacting
dailyNews.description: Former Colorado Teacher of the Year finalist Tera Johnson-Swartz was sentenced to 14 years in prison for child sexual exploitation and cybercrime.

dailyNews.title: Fugitive illegal alien convict on the run after attempting to strike ICE officer with vehicle: DHS
dailyNews.description: Illegal alien Xa Lee tried to strike an ICE officer with his car in Sacramento, California, during an arrest attempt, and the fugitive remains at large.

dailyNews.title: Detransitioner Chloe Cole accuses media of ‘trying to suppress’ coverage of transgender shooters
dailyNews.description: Detransitioner Chloe Cole said on Adam Carolla’s podcast that media and medical institutions are failing to address mental health issues tied to transgender violence.

dailyNews.title: Bills to honor Charlie Kirk, free speech in several states hit roadblocks
dailyNews.description: Republican lawmakers in Oklahoma, Minnesota, and Tennessee have introduced bills to honor Charlie Kirk with statues and plazas after his assassination.

dailyNews.title: Kylie Jenner wows in tiny string bikini as she calls her beach vacation 'heaven'
dailyNews.description: Kylie Jenner shared tropical bikini photos on Instagram, captioning them 'heaven' while showing off neon and black swimwear during a solo getaway.

dailyNews.title: DHS slams California 'sanctuary' county after mom allegedly murdered by 2 Honduran nationals
dailyNews.description: Department of Homeland Security slams Santa Clara County for ignoring ICE detainers after two Honduran nationals allegedly murdered a 24-year-old mother under California's sanctuary law.

dailyNews.title: Jayapal floats reparations for illegal immigrants impacted by Trump crackdown, demands prosecutions
dailyNews.description: Rep. Pramila Jayapal called for reparations for illegal immigrants amid Trump's crackdown, and said those enforcing immigration law should be prosecuted.

dailyNews.title: Kid Rock sparks US Army investigation after military helicopters stunt at Tennessee estate
dailyNews.description: Kid Rock shared a video of AH-64 Apache helicopters hovering near his Nashville mansion, which prompted Fort Campbell to launch a formal investigation.

dailyNews.title: Karoline Leavitt blasts minimal media coverage of Sheridan Gorman murder
dailyNews.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt slammed the minimal coverage from the broadcast networks of the murder of 18-year-old Loyola student Sheridan Gorman.

dailyNews.title: Bulls waive Jaden Ivey after he called NBA's Pride Month celebration 'unrighteousness'
dailyNews.description: The Chicago Bulls waived Jaden Ivey after he called the NBA's Pride Month celebration "unrighteousness" in videos the team deemed conduct detrimental to the team.

topStories.title: NBA's Tristan Thompson supports immigration done 'the right way' in nuanced comments on Trump crackdown
topStories.description: Longtime NBA player Tristan Thompson said he supported immigrating "the right way" but argued enforcement should be handled on a case-by-case basis.

topStories.title: TSA Workers Begin to Receive Paychecks After Trump Signs Executive Order
topStories.description: A memo that President Trump signed on Friday ordering the Department of Homeland Security to pay T.S.A. officers did not specify whether they would be paid on a regular schedule.

topStories.title: Senators Seek Answers From Trump Administration About Airport Immigration Arrests
topStories.description: Airport security officials have been sharing passenger data with immigration agents, but the program received little attention until videos captured a woman and her daughter being detained at San Francisco’s airport.

topStories.title: Mexico Pressures U.S. Over Deaths of Its Citizens in ICE Custody
topStories.description: Claudia Sheinbaum, president of Mexico, said her country would take legal steps to demand better conditions at immigration detention facilities, where she said 14 Mexican citizens had died since President Trump took office.

topStories.title: After Months of Threats, Trump Softens His Stance on Blocking Oil to Cuba
topStories.description: The White House press secretary said the United States would evaluate oil shipments to Cuba on a “case-by-case basis,” after a Russian tanker full of crude reached the island.

topStories.title: DeSantis Signs Bill to Rename Florida Airport for Trump
topStories.description: The airport near West Palm Beach will be rebranded the President Donald J. Trump International Airport on July 1, if approved by the Federal Aviation Administration.

topStories.title: Trump Reveals New Look for White House Ballroom After Times Article
topStories.description: Architects and preservation organizations have warned that little scrutiny has been given to the project’s design plans.

dailyNews.title: ‘I Think That MAGA Is Dying:’ Inside the Youth Movement at CPAC
dailyNews.description: At a sparsely attended Conservative Political Action Conference, young Republicans were eager to start the post-Trump era.

dailyNews.title: States Plow Ahead With A.I. Regulation, Defying Trump
dailyNews.description: States ranging from California to Utah are taking steps to place guardrails on the technology even after the president ordered them to stop.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens to invade Iran, "obliterate" its energy resources
dailyNews.description: Energy markets remain volatile as President Trump threatens Iran with an invasion to seize its oil while also suggesting a deal could soon end the war.

dailyNews.title: 3/23: Face the Nation
dailyNews.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," National Security Adviser Mike Waltz and Senate Homeland Security chair Rand Paul join as the backlash grows against the Trump administration for the deportation of alleged Venezuelan gang members. Plus. House intelligence committee member Democratic Rep. Jim Himes joins.

dailyNews.title: DeSantis signs bill to rename Palm Beach airport after Trump
dailyNews.description: President Trump's family business filed a trademark application for the name in February in response to the Florida bill.

dailyNews.title: Special Operations Forces, Marines and Army troops now in Middle East, sources say
dailyNews.description: The forces give Trump military options in Iran, including operations that could target opening the Strait of Hormuz, take oil from Kharg Island or seize Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium, sources said.

dailyNews.title: TSA workers start receiving paychecks after weeks of financial strain
dailyNews.description: TSA workers across the country are starting to receive about a month's worth of back pay after President Trump signed an executive order last week. Kris Van Cleave reports.

dailyNews.title: Oil sees choppy trading as traders parse Trump comments on Iran war
dailyNews.description: Oil prices fell on Tuesday as U.S. President Donald Trump reportedly expressed willingness to end the military hostilities against Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely shut.

dailyNews.title: Asia markets mostly fall as Trump comments on Iran war stoke oil volatility
dailyNews.description: Asia-Pacific markets largely fell on Tuesday as investors assesed Mideast conflict and volatile crude oil prices.

dailyNews.title: Are there talks to end the Iran war? Depends on which side you ask
dailyNews.description: It has been more than a week since President Donald Trump announced the U.S. was in conversation with Iran. But Tehran denies negotiations are taking place .

dailyNews.title: White House questioned about Trump's threat to target Iranian civilian infrastructure
dailyNews.description: During a press briefing, NBC News' Garrett Haake questioned White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt about President Trump's recent social media post threatening to target Iranian infrastructure, including electric generating and water desalinization plants.

dailyNews.title: WH pressed on Trump’s threat to hit civilian infrastructure in Iran
dailyNews.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Donald Trump threatened the destruction of Iran's civilian energy and water infrastructure if a deal to end the war and reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz is not reached soon. NBC News' Garrett Haake questioned White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt about the president's threat.
